english: skábs-su (skabs su) or skabs (skabs) with genit. at the time of, on occasion of, during, while, when; dé-ka (de ka) skáb-su (skabs su) in a moment, instantly, skabs 'dir (skabs 'dir) now, here, in this case, in this place (of a book etc.) frq., skabs-re (skabs re) once, for a time, *skabs-tóg* (*skabs tog*) Ld. (col.) now, bár-skabs (bar skabs) interval, interlapse of time Tar.; dús-skabs (dus skabs), gnás-skabs (gnas skabs), time, state, situation, skabs (skabs) dań (dang) sbyár-ba (sbyar ba), dús-skabs (dus skabs) dań (dang) bstún-pa (bstun pa) fit for, adapted, suited to the occasion. — 2. Cs. 'mode, method', or perh. rather, way, manner; so the word seems to be used in Wdn.: ldúm-bui (ldum bu'i) skabs (skabs) la-púg (la phug) dań (dang) skyé-lugs (skye lugs) 'drá-bar ('dra bar) the manner (nature) of the plant being similar to that of a radish as to growth. — 3. section, chapter (cf. gtam (gtam) no. 3), so esp. in Tar.; skabs bcu (skabs bcu) the ten sections of Buddhistical theology, also: one that has absolved them.

I. vb. 1. = rkám-pa (rkam pa) to long for. — 2. = ském-pa (skem pa) (bskam-pa (bskam pa)). — II. sbst. 1. = rkám-pa (rkam pa) longing, 2. a pair of tongs; skam-čúń (skam chung) small tongs, pincers; also several other instruments of a similar shape. — III. adj., com. skám-po (skam po) dry, skam-rlón (skam rlon) 1. dry and wet. — 2. dryness in a relative sense, *skam-śí* (*skam shi*) Ld. very lean (like a mummy), skám-sa (skam sa) the dry land, the shore, skam (skam) id., skám-sar (skam sar) pyín-pa (phyin pa), skam-la (skam la) sléb-pa (sleb pa) to get ashore, skam-lam (skam lam) journey by land Wts.; *skam-sań* (*skam sang*) Ld. meat perfectly dried.
this and the compounds skar-ka (skar ka) and skar-tśád (skar tshad) v. under ska-ba (ska ba); skar-kúń (skar khung) etc. under skar-ma (skar ma).

Cs.: 'a penning of cattle, assortment, separation, to pen, to fold, to separate'. But as these significations seem to belong to the spelling bkar-ba (bkar ba) and dgar-ba (dgar ba), it will be safer to confine the verb skar-ba (skar ba), pf. bskar (bskar), imp. skor (skor), to the following, 1. to hang up, *skar-tań-će* (*skar tang ce*), čár-la (char la) skár-ce (skar ce) id. Ld. (e. g. clothes). — 2. to weigh, and *skar* (*skar*) weight, *gau ńul gui skar* (*ga'u dngul gyi skar*) a little box weighing 9 rupees (about 4 ounces); *skár-ka* (*skar ka*) or *-Ká* (*-kha*) weight; *skar-tśád* (*skar tshad*) measure, scale. W., C. — 3. *skar-tág* (*skar tag*) *táń-će* (*tang ce*) to inquire rigorously; to restrict, to bind down, to flog; *skar-lćág* (*skar lcag*) a rigorous inquiring, a flogging W., also C.

Ssk. तारा 1. star, fixed star, nyi (nyi) zla (zla) gza (gza') skar (skar) sun, moon, planets and stars; sometimes it is used generally: *skar-čéń* (*skar chen*) a very large, uncommonly bright star, esp. Venus when appearing as evening- or morning-star; nyín-moi (nyin mo'i) skár-ma (skar ma) a star seen in the day-time (a thing of very rare occurrence). — 2. constellation, asterism; btsas-skár (btsas skar) constellation of nativity Med; g-yań-skár (g.yang skar) propitious constellation (such are the nakśatras no. 4 to 27 v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar)). Comp. skár-kuń (skar khung) (the same word as dkar-kuń (dkar khung), but of a different etymology) window. — skar-kóńs (skar khongs) Cs.: 'the angular distance between two stars or planets' (?) — skar-lńá (skar lnga) a weight ('5 points' on the steelyard for gold) = 1 žo (zho) or 1/10 ounce; as money = 1/3 rupee. — skar-čú (skar chu) 'star-water'; bathing under the constellation skár-ma rib-či (skar ma rib ci) (prob. rewati, the 28th nakśatra, is meant) in October is considered beneficial for every kind of complaint, because Sańs-rgyás (sangs rgyas) smán-pai (sman pa'i) rgyál-po (rgyal po) (quasi 'Buddha Aesculapius', to whom the origin of the medical science is ascribed by Tibetan Buddhists), bathed in that season, and therefore Tibetans, though not particularly fond of washing and bathing in general, are said to follow this example pretty frequetnly. — skar-mdá (skar mda') (Cs.: 'ignis fatuus'?) a shooting star, ltuń (ltung) or sa-la (sa la) dril (dril) is coming down, phańs (phangs) Mil. id. — skar-dpyád (skar dpyad), -rtsís (-rtsis) astrology. — skar-prán (skar phran) a small star. — skar-tsógs (skar tshogs) the starry host. — skar-odzin (skar 'dzin) 'star-catching', making one's self sure of a propitious constellation, e. g. for an intended journey, by a sham departure, conveying luggage or goods to the next village etc., but then
